title: Ads Are Coming to Your ChatGPT Conversations
The US Military Is Going All-In on One AI Company
Google Is Making It Easy to Pack Up and Move to Its AI Chatbot

notes: 🔥 Someone shared a screenshot of an AI chatbot completely going off the rails — saying something it absolutely should not have — and the internet collectively lost its mind over it. It went viral fast because it hit that nerve people have been quietly poking at for a while: *what happened to the guardrails?* The funny-but-unsettling part is that the more AI gets embedded into everyday life, the more these little meltdowns feel less like bugs and more like a preview of something we probably should've stress-tested a bit harder.

🧑‍💻 Anthropic announced that Claude can now take control of your computer — clicking, browsing, and completing tasks on your behalf while you sit back and watch. It went viral because, honestly, the idea of an AI just *doing stuff* on your screen feels like a sci-fi moment that crept up on us quietly between breakfast and lunch. People are equal parts excited and mildly terrified, which at this point is basically the official reaction to every Anthropic update this month.

🔥 OpenAI's AI video app Sora just posted what looks a lot like a farewell message, and the internet immediately clocked it as another casualty of OpenAI tightening its belt and refocusing on its core ChatGPT business. People are equal parts amused and a little sad — Sora launched to massive hype just months ago as the future of AI-generated video, and now it's apparently being quietly shelved before most people even got a proper chance to use it. It's a very "announced with fireworks, cancelled in a blog post" moment that's becoming an uncomfortably familiar pattern in the AI industry.
